results.title: Noodles beat NVIDIA | Product Hunt
results.content:
## Five AI tools you may have missed

[](

Ellis — AI notes for in-person meetings, entirely on-device. No upload, no cloud sync, just a transcript and summary that stay on your machine. For the conversations you actually have in a room rather than on a call, which most note-taking apps have been quietly ignoring.

Ogment AI — An AI coworker that lives in your Slack. Tag @O in any channel and it joins the conversation, answers questions, and runs tasks across your connected tools — already in the thread when context matters most, instead of a separate app you have to open and brief from scratch.

Pros

AI note taking(2)

meeting transcripts(2)

•1 review

Great meeting assistant, built with collaboration in mind: you can have the transcript and summary, and ask questions about the meeting - super helpful! Works best if the whole company is using it, or at least your department. If you want to just record / transcribe some meetings, there are many other AI notetakers. But if you want to have it across the company, I feel like Fellow is the product to choose, since they are trying to help you organize the whole meeting process: planning before, perform during, and support / store your data securely after the meeting.

#### What's great

meeting organization (1)meeting summaries (1)security options (1)meeting transcripts (2)

119 views

•1 review [...] View all Fellow.ai launches

4.7

Based on 17 reviews

Review Fellow.ai?

Reviewers largely see Fellow.ai as a strong meeting workspace that makes meetings more structured, collaborative, and easier to follow up on. They repeatedly praise accurate transcripts, useful AI summaries and search, shared agendas, action-item tracking, and the ability to revisit past conversations, especially for managers, recurring meetings, and company-wide alignment. Several say it works best when adopted across a team or department, not just for standalone transcription. The main drawbacks mentioned are an initially overwhelming interface, a desire for more integrations, and missing offline support.

results.title: With so many new AI Note Takers - what's your favorite and why?
results.content:
BuildBetter is really good for teams, has a nice chat function that lets you chat across all your meetings and get good insight from your team members, calls, clients, etc.   
  
But for personal, and individual notes - Granola is a champion. Recently I've been using Granola's new mobile app for in-person convos and it's amazing. Particularly for my conversations in Japanese, where the chances of me misinterpreting something, missing a key note, or simply not knowing a word are higher. Granola captures all key points and topics and WRITES THE NOTES IN ENGLISH.   
  
Literal immediate translating assistant. I'm not sure if other's do this, but Granola has been the easiest to quickly boot up and get my notes in a snap...without needing to translate. [...] Elissa Craig
Headliner

Headliner

I have not played around with them too much. We use Google Meets, so the Gemini tool is usually the default. It is pretty good but needs some doctoring before sharing with others—I do think that could be said for most tools though.   
  
Always exciting to see more tools launch. Especially if this produces shorter notes, that would be awesome. There's usually so much fluff you have to comb through to get the more important parts to shine.

Rajiv Ayyangar
Product Hunt

Product Hunt

We can divide note takers into two:

Recording + notes (usually requires a bot to join) - I personally like Grain for user interviews, but haven’t tried many others. My understanding is that sales is the dominant use case.

Just notes - No bot/recording: [...] Lee Roquet

I too have tested a lot of them, and I am on Granola as well. It does what I need, it does a great job, it is less intrusive in meetings as it sits on your desktop and just does its job. I use Google workspace so with @Granola and Google I have things covered.

I have not tried the mobile app but I will check it out. Thanks for the share.

Gabe Perez
Product Hunt

Product Hunt

@lee\_roquet1 yeah Granola is just light and gets the job done. It also gets the summary/notes really well. Not overwhelming notes but it captures everything it needs to.

Elissa Craig
